description.task: Implement a TreeInfo class that, given a binary tree built from TreeNode objects, computes the height, counts leaf nodes, counts internal nodes, and determines if the tree is a full binary tree (every node has 0 or 2 children).
instructions: internalCount: if root==null return 0; if leaf (both children null) return 0; return 1 + internalCount(left) + internalCount(right)
isFullBinaryTree: if root==null return true; if both children null return true (leaf); if one child null return false; return isFullBinaryTree(left) && isFullBinaryTree(right)
height and leafCount are already implemented as reference
The 7-node perfect binary tree: height=2, leaves=4, internal=3, full=true
An internal node has at least one child

description.task: Implement a TreeTypeChecker class that determines whether a given binary tree is: perfect (all leaves at same level, all internal nodes have 2 children), complete (filled left-to-right with possible missing rightmost leaves at last level).
instructions: isPerfect: compute h=height(root); count n=countNodes(root); return n == (int)Math.pow(2,h)-1
isComplete is already implemented — study it
description.hint: isPerfect: int h=height(root); int n=countNodes(root); return n==(int)Math.pow(2,h)-1;
description.expectedOutput:
Perfect tree - isPerfect: true
Perfect tree - isComplete: true
Complete tree - isPerfect: false
Complete tree - isComplete: true

description.task: Implement a TreeMeasures class that computes: total node count, diameter (longest path between any two nodes, measured in edges), and maximum width (maximum number of nodes at any single level).
instructions: maxWidth: use BFS with a queue; for each level process all current queue size nodes and track max count
size and diameter are already implemented — do not modify
Tree: 1→{2→{4,5},3→{null,6}}; size=6, diameter=4 (path 4-2-1-3-6), maxWidth=2 (level 1 has nodes 2,3)
BFS level order: level 0=[1], level 1=[2,3], level 2=[4,5,6]
maxWidth = max over all levels of level.size() = 3 (level 2)
description.hint: maxWidth: Queue<TreeNode> q=new LinkedList<>(); q.add(root); int max=0; while(!q.isEmpty()){int sz=q.size(); max=Math.max(max,sz); for(int i=0;i<sz;i++){TreeNode n=q.poll(); if(n.left!=null)q.add(n.left); if(n.right!=null)q.add(n.right);}} return max;
